meta.description: Experts on joysticks, control levers and precision mechanics. Norwegian engineering trusted by Kongsberg, Wärtsilä, Siemens, GE and CERN.

home.heroSub: A high-tech production company with expertise in machining a wide range of materials, and assembling mechanics and electronics — all under one roof in Horten.
home.clientsLine: Kongsberg Maritime · Wärtsilä · Siemens · General Electric · CERN
home.industriesTitle: Built for the most demanding environments on — and off — earth.
home.industriesSub: From ship bridges crossing the North Sea to the Large Hadron Collider, our components hold tolerances measured in thousandths of a millimetre.
home.caseTitle: The Large Hadron Collider needed accuracy down to hundredths of a millimetre. We delivered.
home.ctaTitle: We design, engineer and produce in-house. Tell us what you need and we'll engineer it with you.

maritime.name: Maritime
maritime.tag: Our largest segment — bridges worldwide run on Lilaas levers.
maritime.body: Supply vessels, ferries, tankers, cruise ships, yachts. Synchronised electric motors let captains move between stations seamlessly. DNV GL type-approved.
maritime.applications: Supply vessels | Ferries | Tankers | Cruise ships | Yachts | Offshore platforms
maritime.certifications: DNV GL type-approved | EMC (marine) | Vibration tested | IP65 rated

defence.name: Defence
defence.tag: Components that pass vibration, humidity and EMI type-testing.
defence.body: Mission-critical subcomponents for demanding defence integrators, machined from stainless steel, titanium, Inconel and Hastelloy.
defence.applications: Vehicle control systems | Naval auxiliaries | Ground station consoles | Type-approved subassemblies
defence.certifications: Type-tested | Environmental sealing | EMI / EMC approved

medical.name: Medical
medical.body: Precision components from under 1 mm up to 200 mm in diameter, tolerances down to a few thousandths of a millimetre.
medical.applications: Surgical instrumentation housings | Diagnostic equipment parts | Precision sub-millimetre components

space.name: Space
space.tag: From the Large Hadron Collider to satellite hardware.
space.body: When projects require accuracy measured in hundredths of a millimetre, we're on the shortlist.
space.applications: Large Hadron Collider components (CERN) | Satellite hardware | Test and measurement rigs

page.ctaTitle: Our engineers are ready to spec your next project.

controlLevers.title: Electric, tactile, type-approved.
controlLevers.sub: Synchronised across bridges. Engineered to be felt.

l01.tagline: The flagship. A control lever with an integrated TFT display and synchronised electric feedback.
l01.description: The L01 brings an integrated TFT LCD directly into the lever body — readouts sit in the operator's line of sight, not tucked into a side panel. Synchronised electric motors across bridge stations ensure the captain can hand off or resume control without surprise. Type-approved for maritime installation.
l01.highlight: Integrated TFT display · Synchronised across bridges · DNV GL type-approved
l01.applications: Supply vessels | Cruise ships | Ferries | Tankers | Yachts | Naval auxiliaries
l01.specs: Display ¦ Integrated TFT LCD | Feedback ¦ Electric haptic, synchronised | Travel ¦ ±45° (configurable) | Detent options ¦ 0 / 1 / 3 / variable | Protection rating ¦ IP65 (front panel) | Supply voltage ¦ 24 VDC | Interface ¦ CAN, Modbus, NMEA 2000 | Temp. range ¦ −25 °C to +70 °C | Certification ¦ DNV GL type-approved

lf180.tagline: Azimuth and thruster control for dynamic positioning.
lf180.description: A purpose-built lever for azimuth thrusters and dynamic positioning. Compact, responsive, highly repeatable — the lever of choice across DP-class supply vessels and tugs.
lf180.specs: Travel ¦ ±45° | Detent options ¦ 0 / 1 / configurable | Protection rating ¦ IP65 | Supply voltage ¦ 24 VDC | Interface ¦ CAN, Modbus | Certification ¦ DNV GL type-approved

lf120.tagline: Mid-class lever for ferries, yachts and commercial vessels.
lf120.description: The workhorse. Proven across thousands of installations — supply vessels, ferries, cruise tenders. Pistol, T-handle or knob grip options.
lf120.specs: Travel ¦ ±45° | Grip options ¦ Pistol, T-handle, knob | Supply voltage ¦ 24 VDC | Interface ¦ Analogue / CAN | Certification ¦ DNV GL type-approved

lf90.tagline: Compact lever for tight bridges and workboats.
lf90.description: All the feel of the LF line in a smaller footprint. Ideal where bridge space is at a premium — pilot boats, small ferries, workboats.
lf90.specs: Travel ¦ ±45° | Supply voltage ¦ 24 VDC | Interface ¦ Analogue / CAN | Protection rating ¦ IP65

le90.tagline: Electric lever engineered for system integrators.
le90.description: Designed for OEMs building their own control systems on top of our motion hardware. Full CAN/Modbus interface, configurable detents and travel.
le90.specs: Travel ¦ Configurable | Detents ¦ Software-defined | Interface ¦ CAN, Modbus, analogue | Supply voltage ¦ 24 VDC

cern.title: Accuracy to hundredths of a millimetre — underground, at Geneva.
cern.body: The Large Hadron Collider required components machined to tolerances most facilities can't reliably hit. We were on the shortlist — and we delivered.

about.title: Sixty-four years of Norwegian precision engineering.
about.founded: Founded by Jan Lilaas in 1961 as Lilaas Finmekaniske AS, the company has grown into a 55-strong team of engineers, machinists and software developers — still headquartered in Horten.
about.ceoQuote: We design, engineer, and produce everything in-house — mechanics, software, assembly production — and we test every product before it leaves the factory.

process.title: Mechanics, electronics and software — designed, built and tested in Horten.

step1.title: 3- and 5-axis CNC
step1.body: Fifteen-plus CNC machines, unmanned lights-out production. Tolerances down to 0.01 mm, from sub-millimetre parts up to 200 mm diameters.

step2.title: Firmware & integration
step2.body: PCBs designed in-house, firmware written next door, system integration against every major marine bus — CAN, Modbus, NMEA 2000.

step3.title: Built and validated on-site
step3.body: Every lever goes through vibration, humidity and EMI rigs before it leaves Horten. Every CNC batch is measured on the CMM against the original drawing.

radar.title: Bridges worldwide run on Lilaas levers.
radar.body: Every orange blip below is a vessel with our control hardware on the bridge — from supply runs in the North Sea to Asia-Pacific ferry operations.

cinematic.body: Fifteen CNC machines. Unmanned lights-out production. PCBs designed, firmware written and assembly lines calibrated inside the same walls as the drawing office. Short loops, complete traceability, zero hand-offs to third parties.

productRail.title: One family, from workboat to flagship.

cases.title: Where our parts end up.
cases.sub: From bridges on the North Sea to particle detectors 100 metres underground.

cern.title: We machined components for the Large Hadron Collider at CERN.
cern.lead: The Large Hadron Collider is one of the most demanding engineering projects humanity has undertaken. Twenty-seven kilometres of ring, 100 metres below ground, running at fractions of a degree above absolute zero.
cern.p1: When CERN's procurement office needed sub-millimetre precision components for the collider's instrumentation, they short-listed vendors who could combine documented process control with the ability to machine exotic alloys to tolerances most shops can't reliably hit.
cern.quote: That project required accuracy down to hundredths of a millimetre. We measure every production batch against the original drawing on a CMM before release.
cern.whyHorten: Full-stack, in-house production means fewer hand-offs, shorter iteration loops, and complete traceability. Mechanics, electronics and software are designed, built and tested under one roof in Horten — which is why customers like CERN, Kongsberg Maritime and Wärtsilä can treat us like an extension of their own engineering teams.
cern.beyond: Space, defence, medical, offshore — any industry where tolerance failure means catastrophic cost — ends up on our production floor. We're proud of where our parts end up.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.p1: This website is a concept pitch built to demonstrate how the Lilaas brand could be presented with a larger production budget. It is not an official Lilaas AS property, and product specifications, imagery and quotes have been gathered from public sources.
terms.p2: All product datasheets, technical specifications and commercial terms for actual supply are governed by agreements with Lilaas AS directly. Nothing on this site should be construed as a binding offer or representation.
terms.p3: Trademarks, logos and brand names belong to their respective owners. For questions about use of content, contact the Lilaas office in Horten.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.p1: This concept site does not use analytics or tracking cookies. The only personal data processed is whatever you voluntarily submit through the contact form (name, email, company, message).
privacy.p2: Submitted form data is forwarded to the sales team in Horten so we can respond to your enquiry. It is not shared with third parties and is retained only as long as needed to follow up.
privacy.p3: You can request access, correction or deletion of your data at any time by emailing us.

transparency.title: Transparency Act statement
transparency.p1: Under the Norwegian Transparency Act (Åpenhetsloven), Lilaas AS conducts due diligence on fundamental human rights and decent working conditions across its own operations and its supply chain.
transparency.p2: The annual statement, including risk assessment methodology and identified measures, is available on request to any interested party.
transparency.p3: Written enquiries are responded to within three weeks in line with § 6 of the Act.
